book_claims.claim: 50–90% of US adults are deficient in vitamin D; deficiency associated with increased risk of diabetes, autoimmune diseases, and cardiovascular disease
book_claims.mechanism: Vitamin D is essential for immune modulation, calcium absorption, and cardiovascular health; acts as a steroid hormone affecting hundreds of gene expressions
book_claims.study_ref: ch4-17 — Wang S., Nutrition Research Reviews, 2009

book_claims.claim: Vitamin D is important for normal immune function and maintaining bone mineral density
book_claims.mechanism: Regulates immune cell differentiation; essential cofactor for calcium absorption in bone

book_claims.claim: Deficiency in vitamin D has been implicated in brain aging and dementias
book_claims.mechanism: Vitamin D receptors are present throughout the brain; vitamin D may have neuroprotective anti-inflammatory effects

supplementary_research.finding: Vitamin D supplementation reduces all-cause mortality in meta-analysis of 50 RCTs; strongest effect in those with baseline deficiency
Low serum 25(OH)D levels independently associated with cognitive decline and Alzheimer's disease risk in longitudinal studies

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fat-soluble; take with a meal containing fat for optimal absorption
Optimal serum level 40–60 ng/mL; conversion depends on sun exposure and liver function

synergies.description: Vitamin D is required for intestinal calcium absorption; deficiency nullifies calcium supplementation
Both vitamin D and zinc support immune function; often co-deficient
Book recommends taking vitamin D within a multivitamin every 2–3 days to avoid toxicity risk
